metadata.summary: Link requirements to their origins and to downstream artifacts using a closed set of five typed link roles (satisfies, derives, implements, verifies, conflicts). The RTM is a generated artifact from frontmatter-typed links in source files — never hand-edited. Three separate coverage gates: forward (BR → TC, target ≥95%), backward (TC → BR, target 100%), horizontal (conflicts/derives, target 0 cycles).

content.title: Requirements Traceability
content.summary: Without typed traceability, changed requirements cause unknown downstream impact and auditors cannot verify completeness. Use five typed link roles: satisfies, derives, implements, verifies, conflicts. Generate RTM from frontmatter; never hand-edit. Track three gates: forward (≥95%), backward (100%), horizontal (zero cycles).

why.p: Without typed traceability, a changed business requirement causes unknown downstream impact, tests lack justification, and auditors cannot verify completeness. A generated matrix from source-typed links is immune to manual decay and gives impact-analysis in O(n) graph walks rather than manual search.

list.item: Pre-audit, pre-release, or before a major refactor: needs a single defensible coverage answer
Designing a new RTM schema from scratch: deciding which artifact types are nodes and which link roles are allowed
Migrating from spreadsheet RTMs to requirements-as-code (frontmatter links)
Teaching subagents what "satisfies", "derives", "verifies" mean before letting them propose links
Lightweight projects where generated matrix from typed markdown links covers the audit surface
You only need tool/vendor guidance — use the sibling `business-analyst/requirements-traceability/`
Discovery or pre-PMF: lock-in of an RTM encodes premature decisions
One-shot prototypes, internal scripts, throwaway spikes — overhead exceeds value
Team will not enforce link discipline — an out-of-date matrix is worse than none
Pure SLO/SRE work where the trace artifact is a dashboard alert rule, not a requirement

rule.statement: Use exactly these five link roles and reject any other string at lint time: satisfies (child fulfils parent need, vertical down), derives (peer requirement logically derived from another, horizontal), implements (design/code realises a requirement, vertical down), verifies (test proves requirement satisfied, vertical up from test), conflicts (two requirements cannot both hold, horizontal — requires resolution).
rule.rationale: Without a closed vocabulary the matrix becomes lossy within a quarter; humans and LLMs both conflate satisfies vs verifies vs derives causing coverage errors.

rule.statement: Track three gates independently: forward (BR → TC, target ≥95%), backward (TC → BR, target 100%), horizontal consistency (zero cycles, zero unresolved conflicts). Never collapse to a single percentage — it hides the failure mode.
rule.rationale: Forward coverage is mechanical; backward breaks when tests exist for non-functional concerns with no explicit requirement; horizontal is where consistency bugs hide.

rule.statement: Generate the RTM deterministically from frontmatter-typed links in source artifacts; never hand-edit the matrix file.
rule.rationale: Hand-edited matrices decay within 2-3 sprints; a generator makes the matrix a build product, not a maintenance burden.

rule.statement: Include only artifact types where ownership changes hands: BR, SR, FR, D (design), TC (test case). UI strings, log lines, and helper code are leaves on a TC, not matrix nodes.
rule.rationale: Too-granular matrices become unreadable and mask the real traceability signal in noise.

rule.statement: Switch from a table to a graph view (DAG with role-coloured edges) when the node count exceeds 50.
rule.rationale: An O(n*m) cell table is unreadable beyond approximately 50 requirements; mermaid-cli renders the same data as a navigable DAG.

rule.statement: Every proposed link must carry a verbatim quote from both source and target artifacts; reject any link where evidence cannot be quoted from both sides.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated links are the number-one LLM failure mode in traceability; quoted evidence forces real reading and blocks fabrication.

antipattern.description: 100% forward coverage achieved with one tautological test per requirement.
antipattern.reason: Coverage metric gaming; pair with AC-density, mutation testing, or independent reviewer sampling.

antipattern.description: Horizontal trace blindness — only vertical links (forward/backward) are tracked.
antipattern.reason: The horizontal axis (derives, conflicts) is where consistency bugs hide and is rarely matrixed by default.

antipattern.description: Baselining an RTM during volatile discovery.
antipattern.reason: Formal baselines imply a contract; baselining in flux is theatre and creates change-control overhead for items that should still be under discovery.

section.name: agentic-workflow
section.p: The RTM is a generated artifact that never leaves a human-readable, committable form. Agents propose changes to source frontmatter; a generator reads frontmatter and emits the matrix. This prevents the matrix from becoming a maintenance burden.

rule.statement: Limit each subagent to one of four jobs; agents must never touch the matrix file directly — only source artifact frontmatter: (1) propose typed links with quoted evidence and confidence, (2) detect orphans (no parent or no child), (3) detect cycles and horizontal conflicts, (4) impact analysis on a change request.
rule.rationale: Mixing jobs produces runaway agents that bulk-modify the matrix; separating them enables human review of each pass's diff before regeneration.

rule.statement: Cap any agent run at 20 link edits; require human review of the diff before the generator regenerates the matrix. Lock the source set with a git SHA at run start; reject the run if the SHA moves before merge.
rule.rationale: Bulk-operation runaway and stale corpus errors are the top failure modes; capping scope and locking baseline prevent silent data loss.

rule.statement: Track three gates independently and never collapse to a single percentage. Forward coverage (BR → TC): target ≥95%, measured as (tested requirements / total requirements). Backward coverage (TC → BR): target 100%, measured as (linked tests / total tests). Horizontal consistency: target zero unresolved conflicts and zero cycles in derived/relates links.
rule.rationale: Collapsing to a single percentage hides failure modes. Forward coverage is mechanical; backward breaks when tests exist for non-functional concerns with no explicit requirement; horizontal is where consistency bugs hide.

example.title: Three-Gate Tracking
example.code:
## Coverage Report — Q2 Release
| Gate | Metric | Value | Target | Status | |------|--------|-------|--------|--------| | Forward (BR→TC) | # linked TCs / # BRs | 45/47 | 95% | 95.7% ✓ | | Backward (TC→BR) | # linked BRs / # TCs | 45/45 | 100% | 100% ✓ | | Horizontal | Unresolved conflicts | 0 | 0 | 0 ✓ | | Horizontal | Cycles | 0 | 0 | 0 ✓ |
Overall: PASS. Two orphan BRs (no tests yet) in design review.

section.name: granularity-rules
section.p: Not every design detail or code line should be a matrix node. Granularity affects readability and signal-to-noise.

rule.statement: Include only artifact types where ownership changes hands: BR (Business Requirement), SR (Stakeholder Requirement), FR (Functional Requirement), D (Design), TC (Test Case). UI strings, log lines, and helper code are leaves on a TC, not matrix nodes. Aim for 20-100 nodes in the matrix; above 50 nodes, switch to a graph view (DAG with role-coloured edges) rather than a table.
rule.rationale: Too-granular matrices become unreadable and mask the real traceability signal in noise. Too-coarse and you lose linkage precision. An O(n*m) cell table is unreadable beyond ~50 requirements; mermaid-cli renders the same data as a navigable DAG.

example.note: Link-proposal prompt — five roles stated every call, requires quoted evidence from both artifacts.
Impact-analysis prompt — graph-only; no inference of artifacts outside supplied graph.
example.code: Given the link graph (JSON) and change request CR-{n}: List every artifact within 2 hops of the changed REQ. Group by type. Mark each Update / Review / No-op. Use ONLY the supplied graph; do not infer artifacts not present as nodes. Output: impact table (artifact_id, type, action, rationale) + one-line effort estimate.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated links — agent proposes a link but cannot quote evidence from both artifacts.
antipattern.reason: Require verbatim quote from source AND target; auto-reject confidence below 0.9; never accept a link the model proposes but cannot quote.

antipattern.description: Role conflation — agent confuses satisfies with verifies or derives.
antipattern.reason: Restate the full five-role vocabulary in the system prompt every turn; do not rely on the model retaining it across turns.

antipattern.description: Forward bias — agent readily generates BR → FR links and misses TC → REQ (backward) and FR ↔ FR (horizontal).
antipattern.reason: Run three separate passes, one per direction; combine results before human review.

antipattern.description: Bulk operation runaway — agent edits 500 links in one call with no diff review.
antipattern.reason: Cap any agent run at 20 link edits; require human review of the diff before the generator regenerates the matrix.

antipattern.description: Stale corpus during long agent runs — links point to artifacts that changed after run start.
antipattern.reason: Lock the source set with a git SHA at run start; reject the run if the SHA moves before merge.

list.item: Link confidence below 0.9 — human reviews before merge
Any conflicts edge proposed — human resolves before accepting
Coverage drop greater than 1 percentage point from last baseline

template.purpose: RTM table with forward, backward, and orphan coverage sections.
Minimum-viable generator and pre-commit hook configuration.
